meta.title: Rental briefing
meta.generated: Snapshot generated Jul 11, 2026 · refreshes each morning

ai.html: Bryan Blackburn — picks up the Jayco tomorrow (~10–11 AM). Tier 2 confirmation reply never sent — draft ready.
Willard Schults — Avenger delivered Jul 10 evening (~4:30–5 PM, delayed). In progress, returns Jul 17. Nothing due.

cards.name: Bryan Blackburn
cards.ctx: Family of 5 to Grand Teton & Yellowstone. Volvo XC90 (3-row, 5000 lb tow capacity) with stealth hitch + 7-pin, own Curt Echo Smart Mobile brake controller. Tech-savvy & enthusiastic. First campground has full hookups; second has water/dump stations but no electric at the site — generator confirmed. Pickup 10–11 AM Jul 12 (Sun); return before 4 PM Jul 18 (Sat) — both confirmed by Bryan Jul 9.
t2: 1st CG full hookups; 2nd CG no electric at site
10–11 AM Jul 12 / before 4 PM Jul 18 — confirmed Jul 9
cards.tone: Enthusiastic, tech-savvy about his rig — fine to talk logistics directly.
cards.note: Bryan's Tier 2 fully answered (generator yes, pickup 10–11 AM Sun, return before 4 PM Sat) — confirmation reply still not sent as of this run (Outdoorsy thread unchanged since Jul 9). Trip starts tomorrow — send today. Insurance already auto-printed (outdoorsy-13438732).

cards.name: Willard Schults
cards.ctx: Delivery & setup at Lonesomehurst Campground Boat Launch, West Yellowstone, MT — campsite #22. Jul 10 (delivered ~4:30–5 PM, delayed from planned 3 PM) → Jul 17 (2 PM), 7 nights. Verified driver (Willard); guest Steven D Hague (Steve) is the one using it this year (per Will Jan message). No hookups at the site (water/electric/sewer) confirmed Jul 7. Light contact, friendly — keep it short.
t2: Lonesomehurst CG Boat Launch, site #22 — check-in 2 PM, our ETA ~3 PM Jul 10
cards.tone: Light contact, friendly — one Jan message (friend Steve using the trailer). Keep delivery logistics short and clear.
cards.note: Delivery completed evening of Jul 10 (both delayed leaving SLC/Pocatello; revised ETA ~4:30–5 PM, Steve confirmed "see you in awhile" at 3:30 PM). Outdoorsy shows trip started 11:00 PM Jul 10. No hookups at site #22 (confirmed). Nothing due.

cards.name: Greggory Notestine
cards.ctx: To property north of Cora, WY (septic lines on site). Toyota Tundra, pulls a 28' Jayco, many years towing experience. 3 guests. Pickup Jul 19, 11 AM (checkout Jul 25, 3 PM). Asked Jul 5 about bringing his own hitch — confirmed we provide the WD/anti-sway hitch.
cards.tone: Brief, friendly, efficient ('Yes and yes', 👍) — short and to the point.
cards.note: RVShare inbox re-checked Jul 9 — nothing new since 'Great!' on Jul 5. Nothing due; Tier 2 opens Jul 14.

cards.name: Chelsea Chase
cards.ctx: Delivery & setup at 5136 E Heise Rd, Ririe, ID — week at Heise for swim camp. 10 guests. Changed handoff to delivery w/ setup. Jul 20–24.
cards.note: Handoff changed to delivery w/ setup; Outdoorsy thread re-checked Jul 9 — no new messages since May 8.
cards.tone: Brief, casual; shares plans unprompted — a short warm note is plenty.

cards.name: Luke Smith
cards.ctx: Luke & his girlfriend camping at Madison Campground, Yellowstone NP, WY. Has a 7-pin but no brake controller — offered our wireless Bluetooth controller, which he accepted. Pickup/return at 6785 Valverde St, Idaho Falls. Brief, polite, quick direct answers.
cards.tone: Brief and polite — quick direct answers ('Yes we do!', 'No, we do not.', 'Ok thank you!'). Keep messages short and to the point.
cards.note: Tier 1 fully handled Jul 7 via Outdoorsy — no draft needed. Thread re-checked Jul 9 — no new messages. Phone not yet shown on Outdoorsy.

cards.name: Kraig Smith
cards.ctx: Pickup Aug 2–5 (3 nights). Tier 1 opener (7-pin) sent Jun 15; no reply yet. No other context shared.
cards.note: Tier 1 opener already sent Jun 15 — awaiting his reply, not overdue. Thread re-checked Jul 9 — still no reply. Nudge at Tier 2 if still silent.
cards.tone: No replies yet — keep the opener minimal; a gentle nudge later if still silent.

cards.name: Yogesh Tamrakar
cards.ctx: Delivery & setup at Red Rock RV Park, Island Park, ID (site 10) — Grand Teton trip. Thorough planner: asked about delivery range, amp service, canopy, bedding, kid tips — all answered. 30-amp confirmed. Aug 2–6.
t2: Red Rock RV Park, site 10 — 30 amp
3707 Red Rock Rd, Island Park, ID (site 10)
cards.note: Driver not yet verified — protection package inactive until the driver verifies. Remind closer to trip.
cards.tone: Polite, very thorough planner — answer fully and anticipate follow-ups.

cards.name: Dr. Brad Cory
cards.ctx: Stationary delivery rental at Gros Ventre Campground, Kelly, WY. 6 guests. Renter had generator trouble on a prior rental elsewhere and wants it working this time (6 generator hours included). Will not move the camper. Sep 18–24.
cards.note: Delivery / stationary rental (not pickup), so Tier 1 towing is N/A. RVShare inbox re-checked Jul 9 — nothing new since 'Thanks!' Mar 29.
cards.tone: Direct, a bit particular (prior generator trouble) — be concrete and reassuring; confirm the generator is tested.

cards.name: David Duffin
cards.ctx: Family gathering at their residence in Ennis, MT. 5 adults, no pets. Towing-experience badge on file; driver (David Michael Duffin) approved. Picked up 9 AM Jun 24 (our lot, 6786 E Valverde St, Idaho Falls); returned ~8 AM Jun 30 (confirmed via Quo — texted 'Out front' ~7:54 AM MDT, one day earlier ETA than the original 9–10). Full hookups at residence — no generator/water needed.
t2: Full hookups at Ennis residence — confirmed via Quo
cards.note: Finalized Jul 1: return confirmed via Quo the morning of Jun 30 (renter texted 'Out front' ~7:54 AM MDT; we replied 'Ok. Heading out.'). Final payout $645.60 net ($807.00 gross − $161.40 fee). Insurance was printed (rvezy-1240403).
cards.tone: Extremely terse — often one-word replies. Ask one simple yes/no question at a time; never bundle.

cards.name: Tracy Tremelling
cards.ctx: Sawmill Canyon campground near Howe/Leadore, ID. 2018 Ram 1500 4x4 V8 wired to tow, own brake controller. 8th year renting, towing since the '80s — very experienced & local. Primitive site (latrine only), brought own Honda generator, filled water at home AM. Pickup 7 AM Jun 24. Came back a day early — returned ~2 PM Jun 27 at our lot (booked through Jun 28).
t3: ~2 PM Jun 27 (a day early), our lot
cards.note: Returned a day early — brought the trailer back ~2 PM Jun 27 (booked through Jun 28). Payout $400.50 confirmed (Paid). Finalized as a past rental.
cards.tone: Detailed, polite, very experienced — confirm rather than explain; respect her expertise.

cards.name: Matthew Hunter
cards.ctx: Stayed at KOA, Island Park (Henry's Lake). Picked up Jun 18 (~11:30, after forgetting wallet), returned Sun Jun 21 ~12:30. Casual, proactive, apologetic.
cards.tone: Casual, proactive, apologizes readily — short, warm, no pressure.

cards.name: Casey Frohlich
cards.ctx: Bachelor party at St. Anthony Sand Dunes. '06 GMC 2500 Duramax, own brake controller, Class A CDL — very experienced. Primitive site.
cards.note: Completed rental — carried forward (drops off after this calendar year).

cards.name: Kylie Johnson
cards.ctx: Family trip — flew into Idaho Falls (via Denver) with rental trucks; husband Hunter along, both verified drivers. Full hookups at their site — water left empty, generator declined; had own 7-pin & brake controller, used our hitch. Picked up ~6:20 PM Jun 4 (flight delay), returned Jun 12.
cards.note: Backfilled completed 2026 rental (was missing from the briefing). Post-trip charges collected Jun 16 via Outdoorsy: $36.02 flashlight replacement + $50.00 tanks not fully emptied ($96.56 total charged to renter). Payout deposited in two parts: $833.25 (Jun 9) + $90.41 mileage/generator (Jun 22).
cards.tone: Chatty, upbeat, emoji-friendly — sent proactive updates; warm and informal.

cards.name: Cristian Murray
cards.ctx: Family of 4 to Colter Bay RV Park, Grand Teton NP (Moran, WY) — full hookups. 2025 Ford F-150 with trailer brake controller & 7-pin; retired military (Mountain Home AFB), experienced & thorough. Picked up ~12:30 PM May 26 (after lunch in Idaho Falls), returned May 30 (3 PM checkout). Water left empty (full hookups); generator declined.
t2: Full hookups — Colter Bay RV Park
cards.note: Backfilled completed 2026 rental (was missing from the briefing). Post-rental damage claim filed via RVShare (claim RVSUS3260929) — a dinette/bunk panel was found loose during cleaning; renter acknowledged it and it was handled through RVShare claims.
cards.tone: Polite, thorough, communicative; experienced — confirm rather than over-explain.

cards.name: Kayla DiPietro
cards.ctx: 4 adults to St Anthony, ID (1695 E 500 N) — sand dunes; brought a side-by-side to explore. Own a trucking company and were helping friends move to Idaho Falls. Towing-experience badge; approved drivers Daniel DiPietro & Laura Jean Boersig. Picked up May 22 (11a–3p window), returned May 25 (10a–2p). Used the generator propane and forgot to refill — host waived the refill. Return inspection clean.
cards.note: Backfilled completed 2026 rental (was missing). RVezy's iCal only exposes current/upcoming bookings, so this was found via the RVezy host dashboard. No phone on file (RVezy in-app chat only). Return inspection clean, no damage.
cards.tone: Friendly, casual, apologetic about small things — easygoing, no fuss.

cards.name: Marie-Christine Eleshio
cards.ctx: Family of 4 from California (kids 19 & 18) to Grand Teton & Yellowstone — booked back in Nov 2025 to avoid towing cross-country. Picked up 11 AM May 17 at 6785 E Valverde St; traveled with empty tanks and filled near Signal Mountain CG. Returned May 24.
cards.note: Backfilled completed 2026 rental (was missing from the briefing). Payout $564.97 deposited May 20. Booking predates Tier 1 tracking — towing setup not recorded.
cards.tone: Brief, polite, self-sufficient — looks things up herself; confirm, don't over-explain.

drafts.bryan: Awesome, we'll get the generator all set for you! 10–11 AM Sunday for pickup and back by 4 PM Saturday both work great on our end. See you the 12th!

willard.text: Hi Kaylee This is Will and my friend Steve is renting this year for our vacation at Lonesomehurst Thanks
Hey Will, thanks for letting me know! Hope you both have an awesome trip to Lonesomehurst!

Hi Willard! Just want to make sure we have everything set for your trip. We'll be delivering and setting up at Lonesomehurst on Friday the 10th — if I remember correctly, your site does not have hookups (water, electric, sewer), correct? Also, do you have your site number yet, and what time is check-in at the campground?

Hi Kaylee, Thanks for reaching out. Yes no hookups at this site. We are in campsite #22 and check in is at 2:00 but we are leaving SLC around 10:00 so we'll get there about 3:00. do you want to be there to show us the trailer and the workings of it? Thanks

Hi Willard! Sounds good, campsite #22 it is. Yes, I'll plan to be there when you arrive around 3:00 to get everything set up and walk you through how the trailer works. Could you shoot me a text at 208-516-3380 when you head out from SLC so I have an updated ETA? Just want to make sure I'm not sitting there too early if you end up running behind. See you Friday!
Sounds great we'll keep you updated. Thanks
We got delayed but are now on our way. ETA shows about 4pm.
We're also delayed probably 4:30- 5:00 just leaving Pocatello
Thx for updating, see you in awhile😎

bryan.text: Hi! Does this travel trailer have propane fueled refrigerator and heat?
Hi Bryan! The refrigerator runs off the 12V battery kept charged by the solar panels; the furnace runs on propane. So you'll be in good shape whether you have hookups or not!
Family of 5. Going to Teton and Yellowstone. We have a Volvo XC90 Recharge. I've towed before with my BMW X5 PHEV but the Volvo has an extra row! Should be ok hooking up but help would be great!
Hi Bryan! We're happy to help with the hookup at pickup — we provide the weight distribution and anti-sway hitch. Do you have a 7-pin connector? And a trailer brake controller?
We'll take my wife's Volvo XC90 (3 rows). It tows up to 5000 lbs. I'm putting a stealth hitch on with a 7 pin connector. I have a Curt Echo Smart Mobile brake controller. Works great.
Perfect, sounds like you're all set! We'll be in touch as your trip gets closer. Feel free to reach out if you have any questions in the meantime!

Hi Bryan! Trip's almost here — just want to make sure we have everything set. Do the campgrounds you'll be staying at have full hookups (water, sewer & electric)? Also, what time are you thinking for pickup on Sunday, and about what time do you plan to return on the 18th?

Hi Kaylee. The first campground has full hookup. The second has water and dump stations at the campground but not at each site. My understanding is that the trailer has solar panels which can keep the refrigerator running and also propane heating for the evenings based on your past messages. We will drive to Pinedale WY on Saturday and stay in a hotel. And then it is only about 3h to your address. So what's the earliest we can pickup on Sunday, 7/12? What's latest we can return on 7/18? We will likely stay in Pinedale or Rock Springs on 7/18 and it's about a 3-4h drive. We may hang out a bit in Idaho Falls before heading back to WY.

You can pick up as early as 7am on the 12th. For return on the 18th, we're flexible — just prefer no later than 4pm if possible. Since your second campground won't have electric at the site, would you like to add the generator? You get 1 hour per night free, then it's $3/hour after that. It also comes with a full 5-gallon propane tank.
Yes to the generator please. On Sunday we will plan to pick up between 10-11am if that works for you. On Saturday, we can return mid afternoon, Should be before 4pm.

luke.text: My girlfriend and I are going camping at Madison Campground in Yellowstone national park, Wyoming.
My girlfriend and I will be camping at Madison Campground in Yellowstone. From July 26- Aug 1
Hi Luke! Thanks so much for booking — we're excited to get you set up for a great trip! Quick question before your trip: do you have a 7-pin connector on your tow vehicle?
Perfect! Do you have a trailer brake controller as well?
No problem — we have a wireless brake controller you're welcome to use. It connects through your 7-pin and pairs to your phone via Bluetooth. We'll reach out again when it gets closer.

greggory.text: Toyota Tundra. My wife and I have pulled trailers for many years. Pulling to our property north of Cora, WY. We have septic lines on our property.
Hi, thanks for all the information! We'll reach out when it gets closer. Let us know if you have any questions.
Any chance we can pick up the trailer on Saturday, June 18, rather than the 19th? If so, how much is the extra day?
Hi Gregg! Unfortunately the 18th won't work — we have another rental returning that day and won't have time to get it ready.
OK! Thanks for the quick follow up.
Hi Gregg! Quick question ahead of your trip — do you have a 7-pin connector on your tow vehicle? If so, do you also have a trailer brake controller?
Yes and yes. We pull a 28' Jayco and drive a Toyota Tundra.
Great, you're all set on that end! We'll reach out again when it gets closer.
Hi Zach. Do you need me to bring my hitch or do you have a hitch set-up with sway bars?
No need to bring your own — we provide the hitch, and it includes sway bars, so you're all set there.

chelsea.text: We are going up to heise for swim camp for the week!
Chelsea changed the handoff to delivery with set-up at Ririe, ID (+$75).

kraig.text: Hi Kraig! Thanks so much for booking — we're excited to get you set up for a great trip! Quick question before your trip: do you have a 7-pin connector on your tow vehicle?

yogesh.text: Planning Grand Teton, Aug 2-6. Do you offer delivery to Gros Ventre Campground? Also, is it ok to have 8 occupants (4 kids) if the campground says 6?
Hi Yogesh, yes we deliver to Gros Ventre Campground. On occupancy, I'd reach out to the campground directly to confirm their rules for your group size.
Can you suggest family-friendly sites with good views? Can you deliver to Fireside Buffalo Valley RV Park? Is this RV 30-amp or 50-amp? Do you deliver to Red Rock RV Park?
Our RV is 30-amp. Red Rock RV Park (3707 Red Rock Rd, Island Park) is within our delivery range.
Questions: how big is the canopy (fit 4 adults + 3 kids)? Is there a firepit? Washer/dryer? Could you provide extra sheets/blankets? Any recommendations for staying in an RV with kids?

Great questions! Full-size retractable awning (covers 4 adults + a few kids); no firepit (campgrounds usually have them); no washer/dryer onboard; we provide sheets/pillows for the queen and both bunks plus a couple extra blankets; for kids — outdoor shoes, sunscreen, bug spray, a few activities; roasting sticks & marshmallows included; TV/DVD with power hookups.
Thank you for the tips. Confirmed Red Rock RV park has laundry facilities so we are good.
Sounds good! Feel free to reach out if anything else comes up. We'll be in touch again closer to your trip!

matthew.text: Hey Matthew, this is Kaylee! I messaged you on Outdoorsy but wanted to reach out directly too. Since your trip starts tomorrow, does your site have full hookups (water, sewer & electric)? Also, what time for pickup, and about what time do you plan to return?
I think it does. We're staying at a KOA in Island park by Henry's Lake.

Thanks! The reason we ask is so we know whether to offer a generator or pre-fill the fresh water tank. If you need either just let us know. Also, what time for pickup tomorrow, and about what time do you plan to return Sunday?
Between 10:30-11:00 for pickup if that works. Returning between 12:30-1:00pm on Sunday. Does that work?
That works perfectly! We'll see you tomorrow between 10:30 and 11:00. Unless you tell us differently, we'll leave the water tank empty.
I am so sorry, but i left my wallet at home. Ill send you an ETA as soon as i get it, but its looking like we will be there around 11:30
Trip complete — returned Sunday Jun 21. Final payout $341.25 deposited Jun 23.

casey.text: Hey Casey! Just want to make sure we have things set up for you before you get here tomorrow. Does your campsite have full hookups (water, sewer & electric)?
No we are staying in a primitive spot but do have an rv dump and water available at the park but all the full hook up spots were reserved

Got it! Since you won't have electric hookups, would you like to add the generator? 1 hour per night free, then $3/hour — comes with a full 5-gallon propane tank. Also, would you like us to pre-fill the fresh water tank?
I'm bringing my own generator so we're good there and yeah if it's no trouble it'll save me a stop to fill up the water!

cristian.text: We booked GTNP 26-30 May, coming from Boise — want to pick up after lunch. How much flexibility on pickup? Can you give a more detailed pickup location (going through Alpine)?
Hi Cristian, yes we can be flexible — after lunch is fine, we'll coordinate exact time closer. We're in Idaho Falls just off Valverde St.
Booking now. I have a 2025 F150 with selectable drop hitch. Considering bringing bikes on our own rack — any weight restrictions on the trailer's hitch?
We have a weight-distribution / anti-sway hitch you're welcome to use (2-inch receiver). The rear receiver is rated to 200 lbs tongue weight — keep rack + bikes under that.
Staying at Colter Bay RV Park in GTNP — full hookups, so empty water tank works. Thanks for asking.

Perfect, empty makes sense with full hookups. Does the F-150 have a trailer brake controller and 7-pin? And since you'll have hookups, you probably won't need the generator — let me know if you'd like it as backup.
It has both — more towing gadgets than I've ever seen. No need for the generator, we'll be established in the campground the whole trip.

Hi Cristian! During post-rental cleaning we found a dinette/bunk panel knocked loose — not caught at the quick handoff. We've filed an RVShare claim so it's handled properly. Glad you otherwise enjoyed the trip!

Thanks for the explanation. The weather caused a cursory walkthrough and it was missed — stuff was moving around and it must've been pushed in during pack up. The claim lady reached out, I think she'll get it sorted. Sorry for the problems.

kayla.text: My husband and I own a trucking company and are moving some friends to Idaho Falls and decided to bring our side by side to go exploring, but aren't able to bring our own fifth wheel.
Did everything on your end go through? It said it was paid and all was good on my end.
Yep, everything looks good on my end too. Thanks!
What time are you thinking for pickup? Just trying to get a rough plan together on my end.
Sorry about the propane. We did talk about it this morning and forgot. Are you able to take it out of the deposit?
No worries. We don't require you to fill them! :)

kylie.text: Hi there! I just booked your camper for June 4th-12th and we're so excited! We will be picking up — do you have a hitch you provide for an extra fee or do we need to plan a stop on the way from the airport? Also it was stuck on 11am pickup but we don't fly in until 4, so closer to 5 hopefully.
Thanks for reaching out! Yes we have a hitch it will come with (no extra fee). As far as pick/drop off times we're pretty flexible — just keep us updated.
Can't wait for Thursday! What is there cooking equipment wise in the kitchen? Pots, pans, crock pot? Trying to make a Walmart pick up order! 😊
We have a basic set of pots and pans — skillets and saucepots with lids, plus cooking utensils. No crock pot, so bring one if you need it.

Before tomorrow: our pickup address has changed — 6785 East Valverde Street. Also: full hookups at your site? Fill the fresh water tank? Do you have a brake controller and 7-pin? Want the generator (1 hr/night free, then $3/hr)?
Yes we have full hook ups, no thank you on filling it — we'll fill there! Yes on the brakes / 7-pin. No thank you on the generator with having power hook ups! See yall tomorrow!
Flight delayed out of Denver to 2:45… We just landed!! … We are 21 minutes away
We pulled the trailer to the side of the street at the bottom of the hill since it's more flat. You should see it when you pull up! Here is our phone number 208-516-3380.
Post-trip charges (automated): $36.02 flashlight replacement + $50.00 black/grey tanks not completely emptied — $96.56 total charged.

mceleshio.text: Planning our trip to Grand Tetons and Yellowstone next May — husband and 2 kids (19 and 18). We live in California and would rather not travel with a trailer the entire way. How long in advance do we need to book?
I don't blame you, that would be a really long drive. There isn't a set timeframe — as long as it's available on the site you're good to book.
Booking confirmed — Family trip to Grand Tetons and Yellowstone.
Hello. Is 11AM tomorrow a good time to pickup trailer?
Yes that works. The pickup address didn't change on your end — we have moved: 6785 E Valverde St, Idaho Falls, ID 83401.
Would you like us to fill up the water tank?
I just looked it up — we will travel with empty tanks and fill up when we get to camp. Thanks!

bradcory.text: Renter's Destination: Kelly, WY. The timing should work fine. We actually a couple years ago rented an RV for the same campground. We had issues with the generator. It is my hope the generator works correctly and there are no problems this time around as it was a huge inconvenience. We don’t plan to move the camper.
Added on the two extra nights.
Reservation changes accepted: end date 09/24/2026, +2 nights, 6 generator hours included. New total $1,712.44.

flags: All 6 iCal feeds pulled (RVShare + Outdoorsy via curl; RVezy Avenger + Jayco via Chrome — both empty, no active RVezy bookings). No new bookings, no cancellations.
Quo inbox (+1 208-516-3380) — only activity since Jul 9 was Willard/Steve delivery-day coordination on Jul 10 (delay + ETA + thanks).

Outdoorsy dashboard checked Jul 11 — Bryan still awaiting our Tier 2 confirmation reply (his Jul 9 message is still the latest in-thread); Willard, Chelsea, Luke, Kraig, Yogesh unchanged since Jul 7. New unrelated inquiry from Jessie M (2027 elopement trip) — not a booking, no action.
RVShare inbox checked Jul 11 — no new messages (Greggory since Jul 5, Brad Cory since Mar 29).

Willard: Outdoorsy shows "Trip started" 11:00 PM Jul 10; Quo texts confirm delayed delivery completed ~4:30–5 PM Jul 10 (Steve: "see you in awhile"). Card moved to In Progress — not finalized as past (returns Jul 17).
Bryan's Tier 2 confirmation draft carried from prior run (still unsent) — trip starts tomorrow (Jul 12), sending today closes the loop.
Insurance auto-print: both Willard (outdoorsy-11751184) and Bryan (outdoorsy-13438732) already printed on prior runs — no reprint needed this run.
Past rentals carried forward frozen (8, 2026): David (Jun 30), Tracy (Jun 27), Matthew (Jun 21), Casey (Jun 20), Kylie (Jun 12), Cristian (May 30), Kayla (May 25), Marie-Christine (May 24).
